column1: 0z models coming in. The Northern Gulf coast needs to keep a close eye on this from the panhandle to NOLA. There's lots of very warm water in the Gulf and that translates to a lot of fuel. Should be able to pinpoint details by Friday. Rainfall amounts could very well be extreme across much of the state. We'll be updating 24/7 as always.

It kills me when people ask about travel plans. It’s ALWAYS a gamble when traveling during storm and hurricane season. Be prepared for flight delays and cancellations no matter what, especially considering we’ve had storms almost daily this summer.

That is how we as humans (most who are completely disconnected from body signals) get into trouble. Learn a lesson from this and watch the birds on the last day. The birds will tell you what to do. Follow your gut, follow the birds. What do you think ol' timers did before technology? I wish you the best of luck. And TY Dennis for staying up all night to try to direct us through the storm, much appreciated.
